opportunities.title: EAGxBerkeley 2026
opportunities.summary: EAGxBerkeley 2026 is an in-person conference for the effective altruism community, hosted in Berkeley, California. The event aims to connect individuals interested in effective altruism, providing opportunities for networking, discussions, and learning. Open to those engaged with EA ideas, especially from the Bay Area and US West Coast.

location.display: In-person at Lighthaven, Berkeley, California, United States

display_points: No explicit nationality, residency, age, or academic requirements are stated.
The event is described as being for the Bay Area and broader US West Coast EA communities.
No information about fees, scholarships, travel support, accommodation, or meals is provided.
Applications are submitted through the official form.
No specific application requirements are listed.

opportunities.title: EA Summit: Buenos Aires
opportunities.organizer: Effective Altruism Global / EA Summit: Buenos Aires
opportunities.summary: EA Summit: Buenos Aires is Argentina's first dedicated effective altruism conference, bringing together students, researchers, entrepreneurs, professionals, nonprofit leaders, and policymakers. The one-day in-person event offers talks, networking, and discussions focused on effective altruism and related topics.

display_points: No explicit nationality, residency, age, or academic requirements are stated.
Open to individuals interested in effective altruism.
Event welcomes students, researchers, entrepreneurs, professionals, nonprofit leaders, and policymakers interested in effective altruism.
No information about fees, scholarships, or travel support is provided.
Apply by 15 August 2026 via the official event page.
No specific application requirements are listed.

opportunities.title: PauseCon London 2026
opportunities.summary: PauseCon London 2026 is an in-person conference organized by PauseAI Events (Global), focusing on AI-related topics and training. The event takes place in London, United Kingdom, and invites participants interested in the future of artificial intelligence. Application deadline is August 21, 2026. Specific eligibility and funding details are not confirmed.

display_points: Applications and registration are handled through the official event page.

opportunities.title: EA Summit: Wellington
opportunities.summary: EA Summit: Wellington is a one-day in-person conference for people interested in effective altruism, held in Wellington, New Zealand. The event features talks, sessions, and networking opportunities focused on global health, animal welfare, AI safety, and more. Both newcomers and experienced participants are welcome to explore pressing global challenges and connect with the EA community.

location.display: Tiakiwai Conference Centre, Wellington, New Zealand

display_points: Open to people from across Aotearoa New Zealand interested in effective altruism.
Both newcomers and those actively involved are welcome.
Applications are required to attend, but the direct application link is not confirmed.
Check the official event page for the latest application instructions.

activities: Talks and sessions on global health and development
benefits: Networking with people and organisations interested in effective altruism

opportunities.title: EAGxIndia 2026
opportunities.summary: EAGxIndia 2026 is an in-person conference in New Delhi, designed especially for Indian effective altruism communities and those interested in EA. Participants can expect talks, workshops, networking, and community-building activities focused on EA cause areas and impactful action.

location.display: Aloft New Delhi Aerocity, New Delhi, India

eligibility.nationality_or_residency_rules: Organized especially for Indian EA communities; others interested in effective altruism may also be welcome.
display_points: Especially for Indian EA communities
Open to those interested in effective altruism
Application details and deadline not yet confirmed

benefits: Connect with people and organizations in the EA community
Discover actionable ways to make an impact

opportunities.title: Secure & Sovereign AI Workshop
opportunities.summary: The Secure & Sovereign AI Workshop is a two-day in-person event in Berlin, Germany, organized by Foresight Institute. Participants will explore topics such as AI security, privacy, decentralization, and cooperation among AI systems. The workshop aims to foster discussion and collaboration on privacy-preserving technologies and the future of secure AI.

opportunities.title: Trusted Red Team Network
opportunities.summary: Trusted Red Team Network is an online specialist network organized by Syntony. It connects experts with published work and demonstrable expertise for confidential, project-based engagements in adversarial testing, AI evaluation, and geopolitical forecasting. Participants contribute independent evaluation and systems thinking to influence impactful decisions.

eligibility.experience_requirements: Specialists must have published work, demonstrable expertise, and judgment to turn findings into actionable evidence.
display_points: Open to specialists with published work and demonstrable expertise.
No nationality, residency, age, or academic level requirements specified.
Specialists with relevant expertise are encouraged to join.
Compensation for project-based engagements is possible but not specified.

requirements: Judgment to turn findings into actionable evidence
selection_process: Selection based on expertise and prior work; details not specified.
display_points: Apply via the official page; selection is based on expertise and prior work.

benefits: Opportunity to influence decisions through independent evaluation

opportunities.title: NASA's Lucy Mission LEAP Internship
opportunities.summary: NASA's Lucy Mission LEAP Internship is an online program organized by the L'SPACE Program. Participants gain leadership experience by supporting teams in the Mission Concept Academy, helping structure roles, providing feedback, and engaging in professional development activities related to space mission concept development.

application_deadline.raw: Deadline to Apply: July 31st at 11:59 PM PT

activities: Supporting teams in the Mission Concept Academy as Program Manager
Helping teams structure roles and responsibilities

opportunities.title: EUROAVIA Ideathon 2026
opportunities.summary: EUROAVIA Ideathon 2026 is an in-person hackathon-style competition in Brugge, Belgium, where teams of bachelor’s, master’s, and PhD students tackle real-world aerospace industry challenges. Participants collaborate, innovate, and present their solutions to a jury of industry and academic experts. The event is conducted in English and welcomes students aged 18 and above.

eligibility.nationality_or_residency_rules: Open to bachelor’s, master’s, and PhD students aged 18 or older who are enrolled at a university at the time of application.
eligibility.experience_requirements: Applicants must be enrolled as bachelor’s, master’s, or PhD students. Teams must contain 3–5 members.
eligibility.language_requirements: English is the official event language.
display_points: Bachelor’s, master’s, and PhD students may apply
Applicants must be 18+ and enrolled at a university
Teams contain 3–5 members; individuals may also apply

funding.participation_fee: Per person: with accommodation, €160 for non-EUROAVIA participants or €120 for EUROAVIA members; without accommodation, €95 for non-EUROAVIA participants or €75 for EUROAVIA members.
funding.travel_support: Travel to Belgium and personal expenses are not included.
funding.accommodation: The accommodation tier includes five nights of shared accommodation.
funding.meals: The accommodation tier includes all meals during the competition. The non-accommodation tier includes competition meals.
other_support: Event transportation is included where applicable in the accommodation tier
Participants must arrange their own health and travel insurance

opportunities.title: Syntony Engineering Fellowship
opportunities.summary: The Syntony Engineering Fellowship is a remote technical fellowship for exceptional engineers, researchers, and builders interested in AI evaluation, safety, and governance. Fellows collaborate directly with Syntony's founder on impactful projects, gaining hands-on experience in company-building and technical leadership. Applications are open with no specified deadline.

location.display: Remote (hosted by Syntony Engineering, Durham, NC, United States)

application_deadline.raw: open application Wirth no known end

eligibility.experience_requirements: Strong programming ability (Python or TypeScript), evidence of independent building, clear written communication, and interest in AI safety, evaluation, or governance.
eligibility.language_requirements: Clear written communication required; English implied.

Strong programming ability (Python or TypeScript)
Evidence of independent building
Clear written communication
Interest in AI safety, evaluation, or governance

funding.stipend_or_salary: No stipend or salary mentioned for the fellowship itself; exceptional contributors may be considered for future full-time roles.

Remote fellowship (no travel, accommodation, or meals provided)

opportunities.title: Mission Concept Academy (MCA)
opportunities.summary: Mission Concept Academy (MCA) is a free, online technical training program organized by L'SPACE. It offers undergraduate and graduate students from US colleges and universities hands-on experience in mission planning, proposal writing, and technical skills relevant to NASA and space careers.

application_deadline.raw: Deadline to apply: 8/2/26, by 11:59 PM PT

opportunities.title: NASA Proposal Writing and Evaluation Experience (NPWEE)
opportunities.summary: NPWEE is a virtual academy organized by the L'SPACE Program at Arizona State University. Participants gain hands-on experience in NASA mission planning, proposal writing, and interdisciplinary teamwork. The program immerses students in the NASA mission life cycle, focusing on mission concept development and remote project management skills.

location.display: Virtual program hosted from the United States

application_deadline.raw: Applications for Fall 2026 are now OPEN. Deadline to apply: 8/2/26, by 11:59 PM PT

opportunities.title: Lucy Mission Asteroid Ambassador Program
opportunities.summary: The Lucy Mission Asteroid Ambassador Program offers science communication training and outreach opportunities for volunteers interested in space exploration. Participants engage in public engagement activities, promote NASA's Lucy Mission, and receive resources to support educational outreach. The program is organized by NASA L'SPACE Academy and focuses on Jupiter's Trojan asteroids and small bodies in the solar system.

location.display: Format not confirmed; outreach may be local or remote. Organized from the United States.

opportunities.title: CEEALAR
opportunities.summary: CEEALAR offers a residential community in Blackpool, UK for researchers and builders working on existential risk reduction, effective altruism, AI safety, longtermism, or global catastrophic risk reduction. Residents receive subsidized accommodation and vegan food, with stays ranging from 6 weeks up to 12 months. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis.

location.display: In-person residency in Blackpool, United Kingdom

additional_dates.display: Initial stay up to 6 weeks, extendable to 12 months

eligibility.nationality_or_residency_rules: No explicit nationality or residency restrictions found.
eligibility.experience_requirements: Applicants should be working on EA-aligned projects, AI safety, longtermism, or global catastrophic risk reduction.
display_points: Open to researchers and builders working on existential risk reduction, EA-aligned projects, AI safety, longtermism, or global catastrophic risk reduction.
No explicit nationality or residency restrictions.
Applicants should be actively working on relevant projects.

funding.participation_fee: Sliding scale contribution (£0-900/month) for grantee residency; market-rate (£30+/night) for patron stay.
funding.scholarship: Subsidized accommodation and vegan food provided for grantee residents.
funding.accommodation: Accommodation provided (subsidized or market-rate depending on residency type).
funding.meals: Vegan food provided (subsidized or market-rate depending on residency type).

opportunities.title: Research Affiliates Program
opportunities.summary: The Research Affiliates Program by Syntony is a project-based collaboration network for researchers, security specialists, and policy practitioners. Participants contribute to publications, co-author research, and gain visibility through work with frontier labs, public sector clients, and enterprise deployments. The program operates online and accepts affiliates on a rolling basis.

display_points: No direct application form is provided; interested individuals may need to contact Syntony or follow instructions on the official page.

activities: Visibility through work with frontier labs, public sector clients, and enterprise deployments
